graph.topic: Exploring the multi-sensory diagnostic workflows, visual intuition, and trust challenges of dermatologists when evaluating clinical AI decision support tools
graph.researcher_bg: An HCI researcher focused on optimizing image classification accuracy and system efficiency, but lacking an understanding of the nuanced, multi-sensory realities of clinical skin examinations
graph.expert_bg: A clinical dermatologist who heavily relies on holistic visual intuition, tactile feedback, and patient history, often finding standard AI image-analyzers rigid and disconnected from real-world medical practice

q_01__exp_L1_00.label: Exploring the multi-sensory diagnostic workflows, visual intuition, and trust challenges of dermatologists when evaluating clinical AI decision support tools
q_01__exp_L1_00.description: Interview topic: Exploring the multi-sensory diagnostic workflows, visual intuition, and trust challenges of dermatologists when evaluating clinical AI decision support tools

q_01__exp_L2_00_00.label: Tactile & Physical Reality
q_01__exp_L2_00_00.description: The physical examination aspects that require hands-on interaction, including haptic feedback and anatomical context, which AI images completely lack.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Palpating for Induration
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: You have to press the lesion to feel if there's a hard lump underneath the pigment; a flat photo can't tell you if it's invading deeper tissue.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Tactile Surface & Inflammatory Signs
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: Running a gloved finger over the spot to feel if it's rough, scaly, or ulcerated, and checking for warmth or tenderness which changes the diagnosis immediately.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_02.label: Site-Specific Suspicion
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_02.description: A spot on the palm or sole scares me way more than the same spot on the back; AI often treats all pixels equally regardless of location risk.

q_01__exp_L2_00_01.label: Temporal & Narrative Context
q_01__exp_L2_00_01.description: Integrating the patient's story, history of the lesion over time, and risk profile, which a static image cannot capture.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Velocity of Change
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: I need to know if this grew over three weeks or ten years; a fast-growing nodule triggers a different alarm than a stable one.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Symptom History
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: Asking if it itches, bleeds, or hurts spontaneously; melanomas often whisper before they shout, and pixels don't bleed.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_02.label: Family & Risk Profile
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_02.description: Integrating family history, genetic risk, and overall health status which a photo cannot capture; a high-risk patient changes my threshold.

q_01__exp_L2_00_02.label: Holistic Visual Field
q_01__exp_L2_00_02.description: Visual assessments that rely on comparing the lesion to the patient's overall skin pattern, structure, and dynamic viewing conditions.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_00.label: Phenotypic Pattern Matching
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_00.description: Does this mole look like it belongs on this specific patient, or is it an outsider compared to their other nevi and overall skin pattern?

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_01.label: Surrounding Tissue Context
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_01.description: Looking at the skin around the spot to see if it's sun-cooked or inflamed; high background damage lowers my threshold to biopsy even benign-looking spots.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_02.label: Dermoscopic Depth Perception
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_02.description: I'm looking for specific structures that sit deep in the dermis; it's a gut feeling about architecture that a 2D crop flattens out.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_03.label: Dynamic Visual Inspection
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_03.description: Assessing color and structure under different lighting or angles, which a static standardized photo misses.

q_01__shared_root.label: Exploring the multi-sensory diagnostic workflows, visual intuition, and trust challenges of dermatologists when evaluating clinical AI decision support tools
q_01__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Exploring the multi-sensory diagnostic workflows, visual intuition, and trust challenges of dermatologists when evaluating clinical AI decision support tools

q_01__res_L1_00.label: Physical & Environmental Contextualization
q_01__res_L1_00.description: Investigating how the physical setup, lighting, and spatial relationship between clinician and patient influence the visual data gathered before any digital capture occurs.

q_01__res_L2_00_00.label: Spatial Positioning & Ergonomics
q_01__res_L2_00_00.description: Understanding how body posture and viewing angles alter the visual appearance of the skin.

q_01__res_leaf_00_00_00.label: Impact of skin tension on visual morphology
q_01__res_leaf_00_00_00.description: How does changing the patient's posture or stretching the skin alter the lesion's shape or color, and is this state captured in standard AI inputs?

q_01__res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Clinician viewing frustum constraints
q_01__res_leaf_00_00_01.description: What specific viewing angles or distances are required to detect surface texture that a fixed camera lens might miss?

q_01__res_L2_00_01.label: Environmental Lighting Control
q_01__res_L2_00_01.description: Exploring how clinicians manipulate light sources to reveal subsurface features.

q_01__res_leaf_00_01_00.label: Dynamic illumination workflow
q_01__res_leaf_00_01_00.description: What is the sequence of switching between ambient room light and focused diagnostic light during the initial scan?

q_01__res_L1_01.label: Visual Hierarchy & Scope
q_01__res_L1_01.description: Examining the sequence of visual attention from macro-level body context to micro-level lesion details.

q_01__res_L2_01_00.label: Surrounding Context Comparison
q_01__res_L2_01_00.description: Understanding how the lesion is visually weighed against the patient's background skin.

q_01__res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Background skin normalization
q_01__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: How do you visually compare the lesion against the patient's surrounding skin tone and texture to establish a baseline?

q_01__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Boundary definition beyond the lesion
q_01__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: What constitutes the critical visual boundary of interest that extends past the immediate edge of the suspicious spot?

q_01__res_L2_01_01.label: Multiscale Navigation
q_01__res_L2_01_01.description: Analyzing the transition from naked-eye examination to magnified tool usage.

q_01__res_leaf_01_01_00.label: Macro-to-micro sequencing risks
q_01__res_leaf_01_01_00.description: What contextual information is lost if the examination starts immediately at high magnification rather than a whole-body scan?

q_01__res_L1_02.label: Dynamic Interaction & Temporal Flow
q_01__res_L1_02.description: Identifying workflow steps involving active manipulation or time-based observation that static images cannot capture.

q_01__res_L2_02_00.label: Instrumental Skin Manipulation
q_01__res_L2_02_00.description: Investigating physical actions taken specifically to change the visual properties of the skin for diagnosis.

q_01__res_leaf_02_00_00.label: Visual reveal through compression
q_01__res_leaf_02_00_00.description: How do you use pressure or stretching specifically to visualize subsurface structures (e.g., blanching) that are invisible at rest?

q_01__res_leaf_02_00_01.label: Surface pre-processing workflow
q_01__res_leaf_02_00_01.description: What cleaning or preparation steps (e.g., removing oil, scales) are performed to ensure the visual signal is clear before diagnosis?

q_01__res_L2_02_01.label: Temporal Observation
q_01__res_L2_02_01.description: Exploring the role of time-based visual changes during the exam.

q_01__res_leaf_02_01_00.label: Dynamic physiological response monitoring
q_01__res_leaf_02_01_00.description: Do you observe the lesion over seconds or minutes for changes in blood flow or color that a static snapshot would miss?

analogy.target_concept: Squeezing fruit for ripeness

structural_mapping.inputs: Surface of object (skin lesion or fruit skin)
structural_mapping.logic: Apply physical pressure (compression or squeezing)
structural_mapping.outputs: Tactile feedback (hardness or softness) vs Visual feedback (color change)

analogy.explanation: Just as squeezing fruit reveals ripeness through feel rather than color change, pressing a lesion reveals depth through touch; expecting a camera to see this hardness is like expecting to see ripeness without squeezing.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you press a lesion to check for hardness underneath, is the critical signal something a camera could capture visually, or is it strictly information your fingers feel?

q_01__exp_L2_00_00.label: Tactile & Physical Reality
q_01__exp_L2_00_00.description: The physical examination aspects that require hands-on interaction, including haptic feedback and anatomical context, which AI images completely lack.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Palpating for Induration
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: You have to press the lesion to feel if there's a hard lump underneath the pigment; a flat photo can't tell you if it's invading deeper tissue.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Tactile Surface & Inflammatory Signs
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: Running a gloved finger over the spot to feel if it's rough, scaly, or ulcerated, and checking for warmth or tenderness which changes the diagnosis immediately.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_02.label: Site-Specific Suspicion
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_02.description: A spot on the palm or sole scares me way more than the same spot on the back; AI often treats all pixels equally regardless of location risk.

q_01__exp_L2_00_01.label: Temporal & Narrative Context
q_01__exp_L2_00_01.description: Integrating the patient's story, history of the lesion over time, and risk profile, which a static image cannot capture.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Velocity of Change
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: I need to know if this grew over three weeks or ten years; a fast-growing nodule triggers a different alarm than a stable one.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Symptom History
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: Asking if it itches, bleeds, or hurts spontaneously; melanomas often whisper before they shout, and pixels don't bleed.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_02.label: Family & Risk Profile
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_02.description: Integrating family history, genetic risk, and overall health status which a photo cannot capture; a high-risk patient changes my threshold.

q_01__exp_L2_00_02.label: Holistic Visual Field
q_01__exp_L2_00_02.description: Visual assessments that rely on comparing the lesion to the patient's overall skin pattern, structure, and dynamic viewing conditions.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_00.label: Phenotypic Pattern Matching
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_00.description: Does this mole look like it belongs on this specific patient, or is it an outsider compared to their other nevi and overall skin pattern?

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_01.label: Surrounding Tissue Context
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_01.description: Looking at the skin around the spot to see if it's sun-cooked or inflamed; high background damage lowers my threshold to biopsy even benign-looking spots.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_02.label: Dermoscopic Depth Perception
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_02.description: I'm looking for specific structures that sit deep in the dermis; it's a gut feeling about architecture that a 2D crop flattens out.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_03.label: Dynamic Visual Inspection
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_03.description: Assessing color and structure under different lighting or angles, which a static standardized photo misses.

q_01__res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Clinician viewing frustum constraints

q_01__res_L2_00_01.label: Environmental Lighting Control

q_01__res_L1_01.label: Visual Hierarchy & Scope

q_01__res_L2_01_00.label: Surrounding Context Comparison
q_01__res_L2_01_00.description: Understanding how the lesion is visually weighed against the patient's background skin.

q_01__res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Background skin normalization
q_01__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: How do you visually compare the lesion against the patient's surrounding skin tone and texture to establish a baseline?

q_01__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Boundary definition beyond the lesion
q_01__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: What constitutes the critical visual boundary of interest that extends past the immediate edge of the suspicious spot?

q_01__res_L2_01_01.label: Multiscale Navigation

q_01__res_L1_02.description: Identifying workflow steps involving active manipulation or time-based observation that static images cannot capture.

pivot.coarse_scenario: I completely agree that losing that sense of depth is a major flaw in these tools, but I also need to understand if watching a lesion change over time is something you rely on that static images miss, so we can capture the full range of what AI isn't seeing.

q_02__exp_L1_00.label: The Missing Physical Reality
q_02__exp_L1_00.description: What gets lost when you reduce a 3D, dynamic patient interaction to a 2D static image.

q_02__exp_L2_00_00.label: Tactile Blindspots
q_02__exp_L2_00_00.description: Critical diagnostic information that exists only under the fingertips, not in pixels.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Palpating Induration
q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: You know that feeling when you press down and it feels like a pebble under the skin? That hardness often screams malignancy even if the surface looks calm, and a camera just can't capture that density.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Surface Texture Nuance
q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: There's a huge difference between a greasy stuck-on wart and a dry, scaly actinic keratosis, but lighting tricks in photos can make them look identical.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_02.label: Thermal Feedback
q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_02.description: You can feel the heat of inflammation under your palm, but a photo is always cold. That warmth tells me it's active infection or inflammation, not just a static spot.

q_02__exp_L2_00_01.label: Temporal & Symptom Context
q_02__exp_L2_00_01.description: A photo is a frozen moment, but skin disease is a movie that changes over weeks or years.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Rate of Change
q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: A lesion that popped up overnight is likely benign inflammation, but the same look evolving over six months is a red flag; the image doesn't tell you the speed.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Symptom & History Context
q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: Knowing if it itches like crazy, bleeds spontaneously, or flares after stress changes the diagnosis completely, but pixels don't itch or worry.

q_02__exp_L1_01.label: The Internal Weighting Scale
q_02__exp_L1_01.description: How I mentally juggle conflicting signals to arrive at a final decision.

q_02__exp_L2_01_00.label: Resolving Conflicts
q_02__exp_L2_01_00.description: When the picture says one thing but the story or feel says another, this is how I decide who wins.

q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_00.label: Overruling the Image
q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_00.description: Sometimes a spot looks terrifyingly ugly, but if the patient says it's been unchanged for 10 years, my gut tells me to trust the history over the horror show.

q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_01.label: The Ugly Duckling Gut Check
q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_01.description: It's hard to explain, but sometimes a lesion just doesn't fit the patient's overall pattern, and that dissonance makes me dig deeper even if algorithms say it's fine.

q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_02.label: The Trust Alignment Check
q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_02.description: I don't trust the AI output until it aligns with what I feel and hear. If the algorithm says benign but the patient is in pain, I question the tool.

q_02__exp_L1_02.label: AI Tool Friction
q_02__exp_L1_02.description: Where current decision support tools fail to match the complexity of real-world diagnostics.

q_02__exp_L2_02_00.label: Decision Support Gaps
q_02__exp_L2_02_00.description: Specific limitations in AI tools that make me hesitate to rely on them for complex cases.

q_02__exp_leaf_02_00_00.label: Lack of Depth Perception
q_02__exp_leaf_02_00_00.description: AI analyzes surface pixels, but I need to know if this is flat or raised, fixed or mobile, because depth changes everything.

q_02__exp_leaf_02_00_01.label: The Keyhole Effect
q_02__exp_leaf_02_00_01.description: AI feels like looking through a keyhole. It sees the pixels, but I see the whole room—the person, the worry, the context.

q_02__exp_leaf_02_00_02.label: Ignoring Patient Narrative
q_02__exp_leaf_02_00_02.description: An algorithm doesn't care if the patient is worried sick or completely indifferent, but that anxiety level often drives my biopsy threshold.

q_02__res_L1_00.label: Cognitive Integration of Multi-Sensory Inputs
q_02__res_L1_00.description: Exploring how dermatologists mentally combine and weight visual data with tactile and auditory information during diagnosis.

q_02__res_L2_00_00.label: Mental Weighting Heuristics
q_02__res_L2_00_00.description: Understanding the internal cognitive processes used to assign value to different sensory inputs.

q_02__res_leaf_00_00_00.label: Quantifying Sensory Confidence
q_02__res_leaf_00_00_00.description: How do you mentally assign relative 'confidence scores' to tactile feedback versus visual patterns during diagnosis?

q_02__res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Sequence Dependence
q_02__res_leaf_00_00_01.description: Does the sequence of sensing (touch-before-look vs. look-before-touch) alter your final diagnostic conclusion?

q_02__res_leaf_00_00_02.label: Override Thresholds
q_02__res_leaf_00_00_02.description: What specific threshold of non-visual evidence is required to override a strong initial visual intuition?

q_02__res_L1_01.label: Resolution of Sensory Dissonance
q_02__res_L1_01.description: Investigating strategies used when different sensory inputs provide contradictory diagnostic signals.

q_02__res_L2_01_00.label: Conflict Management Strategies
q_02__res_L2_01_00.description: Examining how clinicians resolve contradictions between what they see, feel, and hear.

q_02__res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Dissonance Resolution
q_02__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: When patient history suggests malignancy but the image appears benign, how do you resolve this cognitive dissonance?

q_02__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Load-Induced Escalation
q_02__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Does contradictory sensory input increase your cognitive load enough to trigger a referral or biopsy sooner than usual?

q_02__res_leaf_01_00_02.label: Heuristic Tie-Breaking
q_02__res_leaf_01_00_02.description: In moments of sensory conflict, do you rely on specific heuristic rules or collaborative consultation to break the tie?

q_02__res_L1_02.label: Translating Tacit Sensory Data for Systems
q_02__res_L1_02.description: Identifying how non-visual clinical nuances can be abstracted and represented in digital interfaces without losing fidelity.

q_02__res_L2_02_00.label: Data Abstraction & Encoding
q_02__res_L2_02_00.description: Determining how to convert subjective tactile experiences into structured data inputs.

q_02__res_leaf_02_00_00.label: Tactile Descriptor Taxonomy
q_02__res_leaf_02_00_00.description: If you had to encode 'induration' or 'texture' into structured data for an algorithm, what descriptors would capture the nuance?

q_02__res_L2_02_01.label: Interface & Attention Design
q_02__res_L2_02_01.description: Designing UI layouts that present non-visual data without distracting from the primary visual task.

q_02__res_leaf_02_01_00.description: How should an interface present non-visual data so it doesn't visually overwhelm the primary image but remains cognitively accessible?

q_03__exp_L1_00.label: Visual Explainability & Evidence
q_03__exp_L1_00.description: What the AI needs to show me on the screen to prove it's looking at the right things, not just guessing based on pixels.

q_03__exp_L2_00_00.label: Pixel & Feature Transparency
q_03__exp_L2_00_00.description: Showing exactly which parts of the image drove the decision, so I can verify it's not looking at noise.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Heatmap Precision
q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: Don't just light up the whole mole; highlight the specific irregular border or dark blotch that scared you, otherwise I don't know what you're seeing.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Artifact Awareness
q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: I need to know you didn't flag a hair, ink mark, or bubble as cancer; AI often freaks out over surface noise that I can instantly ignore.

q_03__exp_L2_00_01.label: Medical Logic Translation
q_03__exp_L2_00_01.description: Translating algorithmic features into dermatological terms I actually use in my head.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Structure & Depth Naming
q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: Tell me you see a 'blue-white veil' or deep dermal structure, not just 'dark pixels,' so I know you're seeing what I can't with the naked eye.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Differential Reasoning
q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: Don't just give me one answer; show me the top three possibilities so I can see if you're considering the benign mimics I'm thinking of.

q_03__exp_L1_01.label: Patient Context Integration
q_03__exp_L1_01.description: Proof that the AI has ingested the patient's story and background, not just the isolated crop of the lesion.

q_03__exp_L2_01_00.label: Temporal & Physical Data
q_03__exp_L2_01_00.description: Incorporating the history of the lesion over time and the patient's physical sensations.

q_03__exp_leaf_01_00_00.label: Growth Timeline
q_03__exp_leaf_01_00_00.description: I need to see that you know this grew rapidly over weeks versus sitting stable for years; a static image misses that velocity completely.

q_03__exp_leaf_01_00_01.label: Symptom & Tactile Correlation
q_03__exp_leaf_01_00_01.description: Factor in if it bleeds, itches, or feels hard under my fingers; a malignant melanoma often whispers with symptoms or texture before it shouts visually.

q_03__exp_L2_01_01.label: Phenotypic Baseline
q_03__exp_L2_01_01.description: Comparing the lesion against the patient's overall skin pattern rather than a generic database.

q_03__exp_leaf_01_01_00.label: Ugly Duckling Check
q_03__exp_leaf_01_01_00.description: Show me you compared this mole to their other moles; if it's an outlier on their specific skin, that matters more than generic rules.

q_03__exp_leaf_01_01_01.label: Anatomic Location Risk
q_03__exp_leaf_01_01_01.description: Adjust your risk score based on location; a spot on the palm or sole scares me way more than the same spot on the back.

q_03__exp_leaf_01_01_02.label: Skin Type Context
q_03__exp_leaf_01_01_02.description: Adjust your risk score based on whether this is pale skin that burns easily or darker skin where melanoma hides in different spots.

q_03__exp_L1_02.label: Clinical Actionability
q_03__exp_L1_02.description: How the AI's recommendation aligns with my real-world decision thresholds and liability concerns.

q_03__exp_L2_02_00.label: Risk Calibration
q_03__exp_L2_02_00.description: Ensuring the AI's confidence level matches the actual urgency of clinical intervention.

q_03__exp_leaf_02_00_00.label: Biopsy Threshold Alignment
q_03__exp_leaf_02_00_00.description: Tell me if this 90% risk means 'cut it out today' or 'watch it closely,' because not all malignancies need immediate surgery.

q_03__exp_leaf_02_00_01.label: Intuition & Bias Alignment
q_03__exp_leaf_02_00_01.description: I need to know if you're over-calling things or contradicting my gut; if the score says 90% but my feel says benign, explain why.

q_03__res_L1_00.description: Investigating what specific visualizations or algorithmic explanations are necessary to validate a high-risk AI flag beyond a raw probability score.

q_03__res_L2_00_00.label: Semantic Feature Attribution
q_03__res_L2_00_00.description: How the AI highlights specific image features to justify its decision using medical rather than technical language.

q_03__res_leaf_00_00_00.label: Mapping Saliency to Medical Criteria
q_03__res_leaf_00_00_00.description: Determining if heatmaps must align with established dermatological rules (e.g., ABCDE) rather than raw pixel attention to be trusted.

q_03__res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Visual Surrogates for Tactile Data
q_03__res_leaf_00_00_01.description: Exploring if visual cues like shadow analysis or texture rendering can act as trustworthy proxies for missing palpation data.

q_03__res_L2_00_01.label: Comparative & Counterfactual Evidence
q_03__res_L2_00_01.description: Using case comparisons and hypothetical scenarios to ground the AI's probability in clinical reality.

q_03__res_leaf_00_01_00.label: Case-Based Reasoning Visualization
q_03__res_leaf_00_01_00.description: Assessing trust levels when the AI shows visually similar historical cases with confirmed pathology alongside the current prediction.

q_03__res_leaf_00_01_01.label: Counterfactual Explanations
q_03__res_leaf_00_01_01.description: Investigating if showing 'what would lower the risk' (e.g., smoother borders) helps clinicians understand the decision boundary.

q_03__res_L1_01.label: Trust Calibration & Uncertainty Communication
q_03__res_L1_01.description: Understanding how the AI should communicate its own limitations and confidence levels to prevent over-reliance or dismissal.

q_03__res_L2_01_00.label: Confidence & Risk Visualization
q_03__res_L2_01_00.description: Design strategies for displaying statistical confidence without causing alarm fatigue or false security.

q_03__res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Distinguishing Uncertainty Sources
q_03__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: Determining if clinicians need to see separate indicators for image quality uncertainty versus diagnostic ambiguity.

q_03__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Dynamic Risk Intervals
q_03__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Exploring if displaying a risk range (e.g., 85-95%) is more trustworthy than a single point estimate (90%).

q_03__res_L2_01_01.label: Contextual Transparency
q_03__res_L2_01_01.description: How the interface acknowledges missing multi-sensory data that the clinician knows exists but the AI cannot access.

q_03__res_leaf_01_01_00.label: Missing Data Affordances
q_03__res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Investigating if explicit UI warnings about missing context (e.g., 'No Palpation Data') increase trust in the available image analysis.

q_03__res_L1_02.label: Cognitive Alignment & Actionability
q_03__res_L1_02.description: Ensuring the AI's output format matches the clinician's mental model and supports immediate decision-making.

q_03__res_L2_02_00.label: Mental Model Congruence
q_03__res_L2_02_00.description: Aligning the AI's explanation style and terminology with the dermatologist's internal diagnostic reasoning.

q_03__res_leaf_02_00_00.label: Lexicon Alignment
q_03__res_leaf_02_00_00.description: Determining if explanations using medical terminology (e.g., 'irregular pigment network') are trusted more than technical terms (e.g., 'high gradient variance').

q_03__res_leaf_02_00_01.label: Attention Map Congruence
q_03__res_leaf_02_00_01.description: Exploring if trust decreases when the AI's focus area diverges significantly from where the clinician typically looks.

q_03__res_leaf_02_00_02.label: Consequence Visualization
q_03__res_leaf_02_00_02.description: Assessing if visualizing the potential cost of a false negative (e.g., progression risk) validates the high-risk flag.

probes.question: When the AI score conflicts with your assessment, is your hesitation driven by past false positives or lack of clinical context?
Which source of bias concerns you more when reviewing AI outputs?
When your gut feeling contradicts the model, is it usually based on subtle texture or overall shape composition?
For a heatmap to be useful, must it highlight the lesion boundary or the internal irregularities?
In a conflict scenario, do you prioritize avoiding missed malignancies or reducing unnecessary biopsies?
Does patient history weigh more heavily than the image when overriding an AI score?
At what probability threshold does an AI score begin to override your initial benign assessment?
Does your contradictory gut feel stem from recognizing a rare subtype or a common benign mimic?

If the training data was confirmed to lack diversity in skin tones, would you discount a high-risk score on a darker skin patient?
If the lesion texture appeared smooth but the AI flagged roughness, would you trust your tactile memory over the visual flag?
If the heatmap highlighted healthy skin surrounding the lesion instead of the lesion itself, would you dismiss the 90% score?
If the patient was immunocompromised, would you accept a lower AI probability threshold to recommend a biopsy?
If the patient had no history of sun exposure but the AI scored high, would you downgrade the risk assessment?
If the AI score changed from 90% to 91%, would that 1% increase shift your decision from monitor to biopsy?
If the lesion matched a known benign pattern you've seen 100 times, would you ignore the AI's outlier classification?

q_04__exp_L1_00.label: Expert Diagnostic Gap Analysis
q_04__exp_L1_00.description: The critical disconnect between AI's pixel-level accuracy and the dermatologist's need for multi-sensory, contextual, and actionable decision support.

q_04__exp_L2_00_00.label: Sensory & Contextual Blindness
q_04__exp_L2_00_00.description: The specific physical and narrative data points that AI tools fail to capture, which are essential for accurate diagnosis.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Tactile Data Absence
q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: AI can't feel if a lesion is hard, tender, or indurated; I lose critical diagnostic clues about depth and inflammation that only my hands can detect.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Temporal & History Blindness
q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: A static image doesn't tell me if this grew in three weeks or ten years; I need the velocity of change and symptom history to gauge urgency.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_02.label: Isolated Image Myopia
q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_02.description: AI analyzes a cropped photo in a vacuum, but I need to see how this spot compares to the patient's overall skin pattern and body location risk.

q_04__exp_L2_00_01.label: Decision Support & Explainability
q_04__exp_L2_00_01.description: How AI outputs must shift from raw probabilities to clinically meaningful reasoning that aligns with human judgment.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Structural Justification Need
q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: Don't just give me a percentage; show me the structures you see. If you think it's melanoma, highlight the irregular streaks so I can verify your vision matches mine.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Contextual Relevance vs. Accuracy
q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: A 98% accurate diagnosis on a cropped photo is useless if it ignores the patient's history; I need relevance to the whole person, not just precision on the pixel.

q_04__exp_L2_00_02.label: Workflow & Cognitive Integration
q_04__exp_L2_00_02.description: The friction points where AI tools disrupt the clinician's mental model and operational flow rather than enhancing it.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_02_00.label: Cognitive Integration Friction
q_04__exp_leaf_00_02_00.description: I don't need another layer of abstraction or screen switching; the tool must fit into the way I already think and work without breaking my concentration.

metadata.question_text: Looking at your heavy daily clinical burden, what is the most critical gap between the 'highly accurate diagnostic AI' that tech developers want to build, and the actual, practical support you despera

main_bullets.label: Could you walk me through your typical workflow
main_bullets.source_question: 1. Could you walk me through your typical workflow when examining a suspicious skin lesion? What critical parts of your examination go far beyond simply 'looking at a cropped photo' of the spot, which is typically what an AI does?

main_bullets.label: When visually diagnosing a complex skin condition, how
main_bullets.source_question: 2. When visually diagnosing a complex skin condition, how much do you rely on non-visual factors—like the tactile feel of the lesion or the patient's verbal history—and how do you mentally weigh these against the visual evidence?

main_bullets.label: If an AI system flags a lesion as
main_bullets.source_question: 3. If an AI system flags a lesion as '90% likely malignant' based solely on its algorithmic image analysis, what specific medical context or 'visual proof' must the AI provide for you to actually trust and adopt its recommendation?

main_bullets.label: Looking at your heavy daily clinical burden, what
main_bullets.source_question: 4. Looking at your heavy daily clinical burden, what is the most critical gap between the 'highly accurate diagnostic AI' that tech developers want to build, and the actual, practical support you desperately need in your daily practice?
